stem: Computers store text using character sets such as ASCII or Unicode.
model_answer: Each character is assigned a unique binary code in the character set. When text is saved or transmitted, the computer stores the binary code for each character rather than the character itself. ASCII uses 7 bits (up to 128 characters); Unicode uses more bits per character and can represent characters from most of the world's writing systems.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe how characters are stored in a computer.

mark_points.text: each character is mapped to a unique binary code using a character set (e.g. ASCII, Unicode)
accepted_alternatives: characters have binary codes in ASCII/Unicode

mark_points.text: Unicode uses more bits per character than ASCII so it can represent many more characters (including non-English scripts)
accepted_alternatives: Unicode supports more characters than ASCII
